examples: pasedomesday.pngThe book itself was called "domesday" or "doomsday" for two reasons.
The Domesday Book was a census, land and property survey carried out in England in 1086 for William the Conqueror.
Mentioned as a royal manor in the Domesday Book, it was long a market center for silk and woolen textiles. Population, 67,300.
Now the rarely-seen community disc archive has been republished onto a dedicated website, Domesday Reloaded.
The BBC Domesday project was one of the most pioneering interactive campaigns of its time.
